feed.title: Nicholas O'Sullivan
feed.subtitle: Townsville-based IT professional focused on infrastructure, cyber security, cloud platforms, education technology and practical systems integration.

entry.title: What Aon's 2026 risk report tells independent schools about cyber resilience

link.title: What Aon's 2026 risk report tells independent schools about cyber resilience

entry.summary: Aon’s 2026 Independent Schools Risk Report provides useful insights for Australian independent schools trying to manage a growing and increasingly connected set of risks. The report draws on responses from 306 independent schools surveyed between January and March 2026. It is important to read the results as the views of participating schools rather than as a measure of every independent schoo...

entry.title: Townsville computer stores — remembering the past and making the case for what comes next

link.title: Townsville computer stores — remembering the past and making the case for what comes next

entry.summary: Townsville has had a long and interesting relationship with the local computer shop. For many years, buying a computer did not mean scrolling through an interstate website and waiting for a courier. It meant visiting a showroom, talking to somebody who built and repaired computers, and often returning to the same counter when it was time for an upgrade. The better stores combined retail, techn...

entry.title: Practical cyber security for small organisations

link.title: Practical cyber security for small organisations

entry.summary: One of the recurring patterns I see in small organisations is that technology quietly becomes critical before anyone has formally decided who owns it. Email becomes the front door. Microsoft 365 becomes the filing cabinet. A phone system, Wi-Fi network, website, payment platform, livestreaming setup or booking tool gets added over time. Eventually the organisation depends on systems that were ...

entry.title: Home Lab Progress
entry.summary: Over the past few years, I’ve reached a point where my homelab operates almost autonomously. Most of the equipment is connected to a UPS to ensure safe shutdowns during power outages. Furthermore, I’ve established a weekly backup schedule that covers all core VMs. I also employ a Helpdesk system (OSTicket) to capture alerts from various services, which also serves as a sort of to-do list. Curr...

entry.title: NickOsullivan.id.au/Blog has moved
entry.summary: NickOsullivan.id.au/blog has moved to the new and exciting URL of blog.nickosullivan.id.au. This move was required to streamline the development cycle of the nickosullivan.id.au site which is now running on Jekyll.
